challenge_text: Start the broker. Subscribe two clients to a topic, leave a third on a different topic. Publish one message. Confirm exactly the two reach it and the third does not. SUCCESS: both same-topic subs receive intact; off-topic sub receives nothing; no error output.

friction_points.description:
Docs (README.md:47-48 and DEMO.md:123-126) state the delivery contract as 'a payload is truncated at its first newline'. Observed behavior contradicts this: a single `printf 'first
second
third
' | pub <dir> weather` delivers ALL THREE lines to each subscriber as three separate records (verified by od -c: bytes are 'first
second
third
', wc -l = 3). `pub` drains its entire stdin and forwards every newline-terminated line as its own record; it does not truncate at the first newline. The truncation claim describes per-record framing semantics but reads, to a user, as a payload-size limit on a publish — which is false. For my use case (a sensor piping a multi-line reading) this is actually convenient, but the doc predicts the wrong observable behavior, which is a claim-honesty gap.
friction_points.workaround: None needed functionally; just don't trust the 'truncated at first newline' wording — multi-line stdin is fanned out line-by-line, not clipped.

friction_points.description: Empty topic directories are not reaped. After all three subscribers exit (0 sub_*.fifo remain under topics/weather and topics/traffic), the topic directories themselves persist under topics/. This is bounded by distinct-topic count (not by message volume), so it is not a leak in the unbounded sense my persona worries about, and neither README nor DEMO promises topic-dir reaping. Noting it because an air-gapped box running for months with churning topic names would accumulate empty dirs.
friction_points.workaround: Periodically rmdir empty topic dirs out-of-band, or restart with a fresh state dir.

wished_for: A one-line note in DEMO/README that `pub` forwards EACH line of stdin as a separate record (the multi-line behavior I observed), so the 'truncated at first newline' wording isn't read as a payload cap.
An optional empty-topic-dir reaper (or a doc line saying topic dirs are intentionally sticky) for long-running air-gapped deployments.
what_worked_well: Core fan-out challenge passed cleanly first try: two weather subs both received 'sunny-22C' intact, the traffic sub received nothing, pub rc=0, all subscriber stderr empty, broker.log empty.
Idle CPU was a flat 0% across two 5s ps samples of all socat/sched/shellmux processes — exactly what my persona requires for battery/idle-power.
Forget-on-death worked: after killing subs, 0 sub_*.fifo remained under the topic.
NUL stripping works as documented: printf 'A\x00B\n' arrived as 'AB'.
All round-001 regressions reachable from this challenge stayed fixed (F1/F4/F7/H1/F2) with clean broker.log.
comparison_to_expectations: Round-001 scored 9/10. The functional behavior is at least as good (fan-out, isolation, 0% idle CPU, all prior fixes holding). I am dropping one point because re-reading the hardened delivery-contract docs as a careful user, the 'truncated at first newline' claim does not match the observed multi-line publish behavior — a small but real honesty gap introduced/sharpened in the very docs that were tightened to be honest. That is exactly the kind of thing my persona scrutinizes.
free_text: Setup via docker run was frictionless. Challenge succeeded on first attempt with no error output anywhere. Regression sweep (run black-box, never reading src/): F4 sub/pub --help both print usage and exit 0; F1 'pub --at xyz' and 'pub --delay 1.5' both rejected with rc=1 and a one-line reason, broker.log clean (no unbound-variable / arithmetic / value-too-great errors); F7 'pub --at 99999999999' rejected rc=1 and deferred/ stayed empty; H1 'pub ../../../tmp/PWNED' rejected rc=1 and /tmp/PWNED was NOT created (verified ls); F2 'cat drops_* 2>/dev/null || echo no drops' prints 'no drops' on a healthy topic. The one new dent is the newline-truncation wording (fp-001).

claim_audit.claim: a payload is truncated at its first newline
claim_audit.evidence:
Single `pub` with stdin 'first
second
third
' delivered all three lines as three records to each sub (od -c confirmed full bytes, wc -l=3). pub forwards every line; it does not truncate the payload at the first newline.

claim_audit.claim: Topic names must be [A-Za-z0-9._-]+ with no leading dot so they cannot be a ../ traversal that mkdirs outside the state dir
claim_audit.evidence: pub <dir> '../../../tmp/PWNED' returned rc=1 'bad topic name' and /tmp/PWNED was not created.

claim_audit.claim: --at/--delay must be a non-negative integer within SHELLMUX_MAX_DEFER_S or returns nonzero with a one-line reason
claim_audit.evidence: --at xyz, --delay 1.5, --at 99999999999 each returned rc=1 with a one-line reason; deferred/ stayed empty; broker.log clean.
Two 5s ps samples of socat/sched/shellmux processes summed to 0% pcpu while idle with 3 subscribers connected.

proof_assessment.notes: This challenge (fan-out / topic routing) does not exercise the deadline-scheduler chaos proof; the heavy N=5000 gate was out of scope for this pair and not run. The deferred-message path was only touched negatively (rejection of bad --at/--delay).
